title: Supporting guests on teams
excerpt:
Looking to add my customers to groups by their organization, 
Thanks

posts.author: RyanBY (Ryan BY)
posts.cookedHtml:
Looking to add my customers to groups by their organization,
Thanks

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: We need the same for our external staff.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @RyanBY - Yes, Guests can be added to Teams in monday.

posts.author: RyanBY (Ryan BY)
posts.cookedHtml:
Cheers Mark,
Looking at the teams members options, looks like I can only add team members (users/ viewers) but not external guests.

Would be much easier to manage everything in the group level rather than individuals based.

image784×400 12.2 KB

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi.
Well… this is correct or dont???

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @RyanBY - something has changed. This functionality did work up to a few days ago and has mysterious disappeared.

I have double checked a few client accounts and they have guests in teams, but are no longer to add new ones? I am going to follow up with Monday to see if this is a bug or a change…

Mark

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: It hasn’t worked for us at Polished Geek ever that I can recall, @mark.anley . I did try a sneaky workaround - I made someone a member, added them to a Team, and then changed them back to Guest. But alas, they were removed from the Team automatically, so that doesn’t work either.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml: @PolishedGeek - very strange! As mentioned I double checked a couple of clients we had this set up for and it worked as of a few days ago. Now an error is received.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: So, we need someone from the Monday team to know whats going on with this…

posts.author: jaacquees (Jacques Barouhiel)
posts.cookedHtml: I have run into this problem also. Would be great to add guests to teams, to manage access rights more efficiently.

posts.author: RyanBY (Ryan BY)
posts.cookedHtml: Can someone please tag monday official support team?

posts.author: jorge.apud (Jorge Apud)
posts.cookedHtml: As to my knoledge, it’s stake that guest can not be invited to groups, please refer to guest documentation

posts.author: TaylorTedesco (Taylor Tedesco)
posts.cookedHtml: Did anyone find anything out about this yet?

posts.author: ElenaK (Elena Korolkova (Kick Consulting))
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey team! Does anyone know how to add guests into different teams?

Would be great to have it a feature!

Cheers!

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Can’t be done currently based on the info in Monday Support articles.

Support

How to get started as a guest

4 min read Feature   Guests are external users of a monday.com account. Being a guest user is a great way to collaborate with a team without being a part of their entire account. As a guest, y...

posts.author: ElenaK (Elena Korolkova (Kick Consulting))
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you! I will add it as a request

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml: Include a reason for adding it. Specifically why you would find it useful. Helps the Monday devs prioritise their workload.

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml: The new Viewers user type allows people to be added to teams but doesn’t give any edit privileges. Hopefully this means Guests will be able to be added to teams too as we’d love the feature.

posts.author: Galit-monday.com (Galit)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Krishele @JohnW @ElenaK that’s correct at the moment guests can’t be added to a team. I’d love to get more insight here to share with our team. Can you let me know a use case for this in your workflow or how this would help your workflow? What does a team consist of in your workflow?

Let me know and I’d be happy to share this with our product team!

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml: We have a few of our Sales Reps set up as Guests so they can edit boards related to their team projects. We don’t need them to be a full user as they’re not responsible for setting things up. However, it would be nice to be able to include them in a group for ease of mentioning or notifications via automations.

posts.author: Naomi (Naomi Brooks)
posts.cookedHtml:
We would love this feature too. Sometimes we have guests who need to be across information just in case someone is away and rather than tagging 4 people it would be good to utilise teams to manage this. Or perhaps an external party or department may have access to a board and wouldn’t be using the board any other way except to obtain information.
I think we would use the tags much more and have a much faster reply rate if this was possible.

posts.author: wyrobot (peter)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a feature of Wrike, which I’ve moved from. Let’s say that you need multiple people to share a board with or multiple people to add to a task. This happens all the time. For example, we have a high-level board with projects, a detailed board with the actual project delivery, another board with special projects. In all cases, we are adding multiple people to the board AND the tasks.

In wrike, you can either add a “group” or an individual to a task or project. This makes sense. Example:

Client - Group: Executives
Client - Group: Subject-Matter Experts

It is time consuming and inefficient to add every person individually. If we could add either, it would save so much time.

posts.author: RobinYllisantti (Robin Yli-Säntti)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yeah this would be a very essential feature.
You could even make 2 Separate team functions.
Guest teams
and Team

I work in construction and we have sometimes the usefullness of having not just the workforce managers and project managers and any kind of manager basically, but also to invite the workforce itself, cause often filling out forms and putting upp walls is a part of progress tracking so we know when we can send in the next guy so to speak.
And when we can send in inspectors.

I takes very long time to put in every person from every specfic company, would be easier if you could save it into a guest team, so if you hire the same company to do the Bathroom tiles again, i can just ask their manager if their team had any employment updates. Most often he would say no, and i could just add the guest team.

Now i have to look up every specific person in the team he has, and add them 1 by 1 again.

posts.author: WyattG (Wyatt Gilmore)
posts.cookedHtml:
So this function was never added? I would love this feature as well.
We work with corporations and government agencies at times with large boards that we add as guests. I need to make it as simple as possible for them to understand Monday.
Making teams available for guests would definitely help so that they will only get notifications pertaining to them and not any others.
These older generations have a very difficult time understanding new software.

posts.author: CoreyBHH (Corey Balcerzak)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature would be useful for email integration notifications as well. As of now if multiple guests need to receive an email notification based on an integration trigger I have to create an integration for each guest. It would be nice if I could group the guest in a team and then only have to create one integration.

posts.author: anonuser73 (Matt)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 from me. I’d love to see this implemented too.

posts.author: thomasdenstad (Thomas Denstad)
posts.cookedHtml: We really need to add guests to teams. Please make this possible.

posts.author: ellias (Ellias)
posts.cookedHtml:
I concur with the previous comments, but w encounter scenarios which have not been mentioned.

We partner with external organisations and freelancers who we need looped into our project management and tasking processes. They users get guest accounts as they are external to our org, and limited usually to one or two boards out of the dozens we have. However, often these users are actually working on multiple projects with us, and are essentially part of the core team, but with limited rights to access our company workspaces.

The ability to add guests to teams would perfectly address this synergy. While on the one hand I can concept of concerns regarding the commerciality of this… the fact is that allowing us to merge others into our platform ultimately benefits all parties.

posts.author: MarkHHH (Mark Laurence)
posts.cookedHtml: We’d also love to use this feature please. We are a drama school, and have 15 teachers who have no need to be able to edit anything, but we put their teaching timetables into boards. It’d be incredible to be able to add them all as a Team into each board, rather than adding each of them individually (ie. adding 15 members to each of the 30 timetable boards = 450 adds).

posts.author: hann (Hannah )
posts.cookedHtml:
@Galit-monday.com I’d like to add my voice to this - it is a hinderance to my productivity that guests cannot be added to teams within a workspace.

I am working for a non-for-profit, and we bring in new volunteers for our many different projects.
We love the guest user feature because it allows these volunteers to only see the project they are assigned to (rather than all of our projects + admin tables). However, the con is that we have to tag / “@” all of these volunteers individually every time. It is frustrating.

I feel that I further highlight the need to let guests be added to teams with these two videos trying to decided whether I should add an incoming team of 16 volunteers as guests or members. The con-side of both options can be solved by letting guests be added to teams.

Option 1: Add as Members
Pros: easier to mass-tag people in updates and on new boards as they get created.
Cons: can see our entire workspace
Video: https://photos.app.goo.gl/wUZRSsRY3gfmNmTg7

Option 2: Add as Guests (which is how volunteers are currently added as)
Pros: only see what we want them to see
Cons: harder to add them to everything / have to individually add all 16 members every single time
Video: https://photos.app.goo.gl/HguVyKxtKS5G9UrE6

posts.author: hann (Hannah )
posts.cookedHtml:
@Galit-monday.com I’d like to add my voice to this - it is a hinderance to my productivity that guests cannot be added to teams within a workspace.

I am working for a non-for-profit, and we bring in new volunteers for our many different projects.
We love the guest user feature because it allows these volunteers to only see the project they are assigned to (rather than all of our projects + admin tables). However, the con is that we have to tag / “@” all of these volunteers individually every time. It is frustrating.

I feel that I further highlight the need to let guests be added to teams with these two videos trying to decided whether I should add an incoming team of 16 volunteers as guests or members. The con-side of both options can be solved by letting guests be added to teams.

Option 1: Add as Members
Pros: easier to mass-tag people in updates and on new boards as they get created.
Cons: can see our entire workspace

Option 2: Add as Guests (which is how volunteers are currently added as)
Pros: only see what we want them to see
Cons: harder to add them to everything / have to individually add all 16 members every single time

posts.author: kevinraju (Kevin Raju)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @hann

As a workaround, if you are trying to solve the issue of mass tagging on updates, I believe you will be able to tag all guests in the board collectively if they are added into a specific board. I have shared an image below describing the same. Does this work?

Tagging Guests2817×1474 242 KB

posts.author: hann (Hannah )
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for taking the time to respond, @kevinraju
Unfortunately this does not work for all of our cases.
For example, one of the projects I’m working on brings in the engineering, sales, and R&D teams from an external company. We would like to be able to invite them as guests to our board, but be able to tag them as teams.

posts.author: GustavoM (J. Gustavo M)
posts.cookedHtml: Any news about adding guest to teams?

posts.author: TaylorTedesco (Taylor Tedesco)
posts.cookedHtml: This is an absolute necessity. We have multiple teams of 5 or 6 on one board and I really need to switch them to Guests but can’t because it’s simply not reasonable to expect my employees to remember to tag all of the correct people every time when we are managing 160+ contracts a month.

posts.author: geminiimatt (matt mitchell)
posts.cookedHtml: I would love this feature. For safety, privacy, and security I am using guests accounts for contractors. We use single sign on for our full user accounts. Contractors are not part of my organization and don’t share our company email addresses etc. They are however, part of the Monday.com board and important parts of the project. To not be able to add them to teams takes away from the way I am using monday.com to implement agile. This is a deal breaker for me and not just a nice to have.

posts.author: TaylorTedesco (Taylor Tedesco)
posts.cookedHtml: Just here to bump this and see if there is any talk of this happening?

posts.author: alona.polishchuk (Alona )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Rachelleigh & all, this feature is necessary for our account, too.
I have 2 use cases described down below.

1st use case:

I created one workspace & 20 Teams to be invited to that workspace. Within that workspace I created 20 main boards & 10 main dashboards. In order to grant access to the entire workspace content, I invited 20 teams to workspace & that’s it, since the boards & dashboards are main, users in Teams are automatically getting access to the content. Whenever there are newcomers in our organisation, we add them to their respective team and that’s it - no hassle with invitations to every single board.

The next step for me is to start “opening” the workspace for externals and here the problem comes → I have to change all my boards and dashboards into shareable type and invite every single external 30 times (to 20 boards and 10 dashboards). This doesn’t sound like efficient way of working. Instead I could simply create one Team with Guests, invite it to boards and dashboards only once and then keep access control via this Team.

2nd use case:

There is another workspace with a Shareable Board A connected to the boards B & C in the above mentioned workspace from use case 1 via Connect Boards column. Since Guests on Board A do not have access to boards B & C, they simply do no see the content looked up from boards B & C into board A. This is becoming an even bigger problem generating data inconsistency for my workflow.

So I do not really know how to handle these cases. I think a possibility to add Guests in Teams would be the most feasible option to fix these problems for my workflow.

This is becoming an even bigger problem generating data inconsistency for my workflow.

posts.author: joehardy (joe)
posts.cookedHtml: Would be useful for grouping subcontractors for us!

posts.author: RickeyCraig (Rickey Craig)
posts.cookedHtml: @Galit-monday.com any traction with the dev team on this? Would be highly useful to help further compartmentalize communications as we scale the use of guests on our boards.

posts.author: Rgould (Rianne Gould)
posts.cookedHtml: We have seasonal employees that we need to be able to multiple boards at one time instead of adding a guest one by one… Need the ability to be able to just add and remove them from a team when they are no longer active employees. They are not suitable candidates for being a member (like our full time employees have access to), they do not need those features or access, so the guest option has worked well for them. We NEED a better option to adding them to bulk boards and removing them at the end of the season easily. This would also simplify several automations and notifications. I feel like teams would simplify this greatly for us.

posts.author: annayahuang (Anna Fry)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 upvote this one as well, we have a network of subcontractors that help us do our work and there are at least 20-40 different people for each subcontractor firm. This is becoming a maintenance challenge to tag them all to the right boards.

posts.author: Frederic (Schmutz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Would be a great thing if guest can be added to teams.
So we could filter the workload by teams.

posts.author: jtho-seif (Jérémie)
posts.cookedHtml: Also ‘onboard’ with this request - the ability to group guests into a ‘team’ would be great

posts.author: Adam.B (Adam)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is an absolutely crucial feature for working with multiple external groups while maintaining operational security without a lot of manual labor.

We have multiple external groups that need to have their permissions closely managed so they cannot see one another’s IP. However, currently, when we add a guest someone has to manually update the view permissions on every item that is relevant to them. If they were instead assigned to a team, then the permissions would automatically be consistent throughout for various external vendors. Additionally, this prevents various guests from the same organization from having inconsistent data available to them due to being missed when adding permissions

posts.author: mf.apparence.io (marine)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there a way to know if this feature is about to be integrated to Monday soon?
this would help us a lot for our projects managment

posts.author: ElenaK (Elena Korolkova (Kick Consulting))
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey team! Does anyone know how to add guests into different teams?

Would be great to have it a feature!

Cheers!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for your feature request! We hear you all and recognize there’s demand for this feature. To increase the potential for development, let’s get these votes up! As such we will be merging this topic into this existing feature request Supporting guests on teams so that the votes can be combined! All votes and comments will be transferred during the merge. If you did not cast a vote on this topic, please add your vote to the active thread above. Your opinion matters to us!

posts.author: KellyHammons (Kelly Hammons)
posts.cookedHtml: We need this as well. In the meantime, is there something in the Marketplace that would function as a workaround?

posts.author: Elados (Elad Ben Shimol)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any progress for this “feature” request?
@Galit-monday.com @monday-moderators @monday-team @CXS @CSG

posts.author: dburton4 (Douglas Burton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi - It would be great to develop the functionality to add Guests into Teams. This will help make the day-to-day management of the platform a lot more efficient by consolidating a number of individuals into one manageable team. It will also give the third party greater visibility of their colleagues’ roles should someone not be available.

Please can this be urgently looked at as a development feature?

Thanks

posts.author: Gys (Gys de Beer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Apparently this did exist but has mysteriously disappeared.

I also need the ability to add guests to teams so manage boards and notifications better!

posts.author: Operations5 (Trevor)
posts.cookedHtml:
We want to invite our customers to Monday to be able to view the projects that we have under way. It gives our customer the ability to view the project in progress, and for us to provide complete transparency. That said, we do not want customers to be able to see other customers projects. To do this we need to be able to invite them as a guest, and add them to a team. Adding them to a team keeps people associated with their company only being able to view there projects compared to all of our projects. I realize this can be done by utilizing a different board, but we don’t want our staff having to switch between boards. We would need 20 different boards which only makes life much more complicated.

I believe being able to add guest to teams not only fixes a huge issue for us in Monday, but it also gets Monday exposer to hundreds of potential other customers. All of our customers are other companies.

posts.author: Patrmg (Patrmg)
posts.cookedHtml: This is an absolutely crucial feature. are there informations about Monday?

posts.author: sharonr (Sharon R)
posts.cookedHtml: Would be great to get this on the pipeline. It would really help organize the external freelancers and agencies we work with, especially when working with an agency that has multiple members that change from time to time and it would be much easier to tag a team instead of individuals.

posts.author: TaylorTedesco (Taylor Tedesco)
posts.cookedHtml: Did I dream it or wasn’t there an announcement that said this was coming at some point this year? I really hope so because it has been such a long time coming

posts.author: mclochet (MARIE-CLAIRE LOCHET)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello there! This feature would be greatly appreciate to have easier control of our outside vendors and freelancers.

posts.author: einavpeleg (Einav Peleg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Julietteb , in last year’s elevate summit you presented this feature as part of this year’s roadmap. Is it still planned for 2024?
I’m currently waiting for this feature release to publish a large scale workflow involving many guest users.
Even a vague estimate will be very helpful with decision making on the matter.

Thank you!

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: HI Einav, You are correct! I just followed up with our product team and we released this feature! Now you are able to invite teams as guest!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Trevor,

Thank you for your feature request - we recognise there is demand for this feature

In order to ensure we have all votes counted to put greater emphasis on this features development, we have merged your request into the existing feature request.

Every quarter we take the features with the top votes to our development team for a response. You can read more about our feature request process here: All you need to know about our feature request process!

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like to see the possibility to group guests as is done with user teams.

i dont call it teams to not confuse with user teams,
but its the same concept/idea, so i call it guest groups.

best,

posts.cookedHtml: I’m ‘onboard’ with this request - the ability to group guests into a ‘team.’

posts.cookedHtml: We have the same requirement - we have shareable boards which we grant access to a handful of users per board. It’d be great to be able to add guests to a group and then grant board access to that group as a whole.

posts.cookedHtml: Also ‘onboard’ with this request - the ability to group guests into a ‘team’ would be great
